allies.rationale_text: Liking-principle helps empathy create receptivity when the audience needs to feel understood before a message can land.
allies.source_quote: Empathy is the mechanism required to build an empathic bridge.

allies.rationale_text: Narratives helps empathy make another person's world concrete enough to be felt, remembered, and acted on.
allies.source_quote: Storytelling triggers narrative thinking mode in the brain.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias undermines empathy when assumed motivations are fitted to prior stories rather than checked against lived reality.
antagonists.source_quote: empathy conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when assumed motivations are read selectively to fit prior narratives.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Empathy conflicts with liking-principle when social affinity is mistaken for accurate perspective-taking.
structured_tensions.source_quote: empathy vs liking-principle: empathy conflicts with liking-principle (liking principle) when social affinity is mistaken for accurate perspective-taking.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Empathy conflicts with confirmation-bias when assumed motivations are read selectively to fit prior narratives.
structured_tensions.source_quote: empathy vs confirmation-bias: empathy conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when assumed motivations are read selectively to fit prior narratives.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on one clear receptivity ally, one strong narrative ally, and two explicit current-vocabulary tension blocks.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor human-centered relation sprawl beyond the clean trust, story, and anti-story-fitting surface.
open_questions: The liking-principle ally remains interpretively lighter because the strongest source block is an explicit tension against affinity being mistaken for understanding.
